title: Unknown XML Objects, Zod Admission, Loss-Aware Normalization, and Provenance · Changed-Schema Lab
summary: An independent university archive receives a different publisher mix, unknown XML shape, validator state, tenant, async schedule, database condition, accessibility need, platform, and injected failure and transfers unknown xml objects, zod admission, loss-aware normalization, and provenance into a changed runtime-validated normalized feed boundary. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of runtime-validated normalized feed boundary with automated and judgment evidence.…
objectives: Keep parser and database JSON values unknown until discriminated format, structural, scalar, cardinality, and semantic runtime validation passes.
Build distinct validated RSS and Atom transport variants and exhaustively normalize each supported case.
Select scoped stable identity from Atom ID, RSS GUID meaning, canonical link, or bounded fingerprint and report collisions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Feed Ts Unknown Boundary
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab TypeScript team handles case 9379 during a PostgreSQL lease and migration fault exercise. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to reconcile unknown parser values, discriminated formats, identity, provenance, and normalization loss; failed, canceled, stale, and cross-tenant work must preserve the prior admitted state; then change one tenant, cursor, reader command, authorization, validator, sanitizer, or accessibility condition without leaking state. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, fast-xml-parser, Express, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, handle and load claims, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?
